title: Tougher Targets
teaches: the wound roll comes from comparing Strength to Toughness: the same gun needs a higher roll against a tougher target.
intro: Two Necron units advance on your Intercessors: a phalanx of Necron Warriors, and a smaller squad of elite Immortals in heavier bodies. Your bolt rifles are Strength 4. The Warriors are Toughness 4 — an even match. The Immortals are Toughness 5 — tougher than your shells. You have two volleys; fire one at each, and watch what happens to the wound roll.

turns.narrate_before: First volley. Pick a target — and note the WOUND line when the dice land: Strength 4 into Toughness 4 needs 4+, but the same Strength 4 into the Immortals' Toughness 5 needs 5+.
Second volley. Now fire at the other unit and compare the two WOUND lines: same rifles, different Toughness, different target number.

outro: That was the wound chart at work. Your rifles never changed — the targets did. Strength 4 against Toughness 4 is an even match (4+); against Toughness 5 your shells are outmatched (5+). The full chart runs from 2+ (Strength at least double Toughness) to 6+ (Strength half of Toughness or less) — ask 'why wound' during any battle to see it. You may also have noticed the Immortals' 3+ armour shrugging off more hits than the Warriors' 4+; saves are the next lesson.
